title: Crispy Herbal Japanese Potato Starch Bake
description: A festive, crumbly bake that layers aromatic herbs with crispy potato starch crusts for a savory snack or side dish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, whisk together the warm water, dashi powder, soy sauce, mirin, grated ginger, minced garlic, crushed shiso leaves, and sugar until well combined.
Add the potato starch and sifted flour to the liquid mixture, stirring until a thick, smooth batter forms.
Fold in the diced cold butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Transfer the batter onto the prepared baking sheet, spreading it into a 20 cm (8 inch) square and smoothing the surface.
Brush the top lightly with beaten egg and drizzle with a teaspoon of vegetable oil to help form a crisp crust.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the edges are crisp.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before cutting into squares.
Sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds and serve warm as a snack or side dish.
